disease.name: Hepatic Amyloidosis
disease.note: Infiltrative disease: AL amyloidosis (primary, light chain), AA amyloidosis (secondary to chronic inflammation), ATTR amyloidosis. Liver involved in 50-70% of AL amyloid but jaundice is a late sign (usually ominous). Classic: massive hepatomegaly + elevated ALP out of proportion to bilirubin + low albumin.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Isolated hepatic amyloid -> SPECIALIST; Cardiac involvement -> EMERGENCY; Jaundice from amyloid -> URGENT (late sign); Spontaneous rupture -> SURGICAL EMERGENCY

historyFeatures.note: From heart failure if cardiac involvement or from ascites
Factor X deficiency from binding to amyloid fibrils

examFeatures.note: Peripheral edema from nephrotic syndrome or heart failure
Periorbital purpura ('raccoon eyes') - pathognomonic for AL

subtypes.name: AL Amyloidosis (Primary, Light Chain)
subtypes.note: Most common type with hepatic involvement. Associated with plasma cell dyscrasia. Features: macroglossia, periorbital purpura, nephrotic syndrome, restrictive cardiomyopathy.

subtypes.name: AA Amyloidosis (Secondary)
subtypes.note: Deposition of serum amyloid A protein from chronic inflammation (RA, IBD, familial Mediterranean fever, chronic infections). Hepatic involvement less prominent than AL.

subtypes.name: ATTR Amyloidosis
subtypes.note: Wild-type (senile) or mutant (familial) transthyretin amyloid. Primarily cardiac and neurologic; hepatic involvement less common.

firstLine.note: ALP elevated out of proportion to bilirubin (ALP >> ALT)
Speckled myocardium, restrictive filling, low voltage

emergency.action: Admit to ICU if cardiac amyloid with decompensated heart failure
Manage arrhythmias - avoid calcium channel blockers (negative inotrope, can bind amyloid)
Spontaneous liver rupture: emergent laparotomy and packing/hepatic artery embolization

inpatient.action: Initiate chemotherapy for AL: bortezomib, cyclophosphamide, dexamethasone (CyBorD)
Diuresis for heart failure and nephrotic syndrome
Supportive care: monitor for bleeding (factor X deficiency), infections

outpatient.action: Continue CyBorD chemotherapy for AL amyloidosis (monitor for response)
For AA: treat underlying inflammatory disease
For ATTR: tafamidis 61 mg PO daily (stabilizes transthyretin)
Regular monitoring of serum free light chains, troponin, NT-proBNP
HCC surveillance: not typically indicated unless cirrhosis develops

refractory.action: Autologous stem cell transplant in carefully selected AL patients
Liver transplant rarely indicated (only for isolated hepatic ATTR with no significant cardiac involvement)
Combined heart-liver transplant for ATTR with severe cardiac involvement

preventive.action: Tafamidis for ATTR amyloidosis to delay progression
Aggressive treatment of chronic inflammatory diseases to prevent AA amyloidosis
Genetic counseling for familial ATTR amyloidosis

disease.pathophysiology: Extracellular deposition of misfolded protein fibrils in the liver, primarily in the space of Disse (between hepatocytes and sinusoidal endothelium) and around blood vessels. Amyloid fibrils (derived from immunoglobulin light chains in AL, serum amyloid A in AA, or transthyretin in ATTR) accumulate progressively causing: (1) sinusoidal compression leading to portal hypertension; (2) hepatocyte atrophy from physical displacement and impaired nutrient exchange; (3) intrahepatic cholestasis from bile canaliculi compression; (4) impaired synthetic function. Amyloid fibrils also bind factor X causing acquired factor X deficiency and bleeding tendency. In AL type, the underlying plasma cell clone produces monoclonal light chains prone to misfolding.

diagnosticClues.clue: Massive hepatomegaly with elevated ALP but relatively preserved bilirubin (early) or jaundice (late)
ALP >> ALT (cholestatic pattern) with low albumin and no obvious cirrhosis
Liver biopsy with Congo red showing apple-green birefringence under polarized light
Periorbital purpura (raccoon eyes) and macroglossia in AL type
Coexisting nephrotic syndrome and/or restrictive cardiomyopathy with hepatomegaly
Monoclonal light chain in serum or urine (AL type)

exclusionClues.clue: Congo red stain negative on liver biopsy
Alternative cause of hepatomegaly confirmed (cirrhosis, PBC, alcoholic, NASH, Budd-Chiari)
No monoclonal protein detected and no features of AA or ATTR
